sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Microchip Technology Surges 45% as PCIe Gen 6 Wins Stack Up: Here’s the Path to $134
feed.summary: Microchip Technology (MCHP) has shown strong recovery, with Q3 FY26 net sales up 16% year-over-year and EPS beating consensus, signaling the end of an inventory correction. The company's $800 million convertible note offering and three PCIe Gen 6 design wins, including one projected to generate over $100 million in 2027 revenue, are key drivers for future growth. Analysts project a potential 41% total return to a $111 target over two years, with a path to $134 under specific favorable conditions.

feed.title: Global Payments Posts Strong Q4 Results, Raises 2026 Outlook
feed.summary: Global Payments (GPN) reported strong fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 results, with adjusted revenue of $2.32 billion and adjusted EPS up 11%. The company completed the acquisition of Worldpay and the divestiture of its Issuer Solutions business, transforming into a pure-play merchant solutions provider, and authorized a significant share repurchase program. Global Payments also provided a positive outlook for 2026, forecasting 13%-15% adjusted EPS growth.

feed.title: Amazon Abandons Blue Jay Warehouse Robot In Less Than Six Months, Pivots To New Technologies: Report
feed.summary: Amazon has ceased operations of its Blue Jay warehouse robot, less than six months after its introduction, reassigning staff to other robotics projects. The decision was due to high costs, manufacturing complexity, and implementation issues, but Amazon plans to incorporate parts of Blue Jay's technology into new systems like "Flex Cell." This move aligns with Amazon's transition to a more modular warehouse system called "Orbital" and comes as analysts predict significant cost savings from Amazon's advanced robotics in the future.

feed.title: DTE Energy outlines 6%–8% EPS growth through 2030 as data center contracts expand capital plan by $6.5B
feed.summary: DTE Energy announced its financial targets, forecasting an adjusted EPS growth of 6% to 8% through 2030, with a long-term target of 5% to 7% beyond that. The company also revealed a $6.5 billion expansion to its capital investment plan, bringing the total to $44 billion over five years, primarily driven by new data center contracts and growing energy demand. This significant investment is expected to support the company's earnings growth and grid modernization efforts.

feed.title: Stephens Adjusts Price Target on Builders FirstSource to $125 From $137, Maintains Equalweight Rating
feed.summary: Stephens has lowered its price target for Builders FirstSource (BLDR) to $125 from $137, while keeping an Equalweight rating on the stock. This adjustment comes amidst a series of analyst revisions following recent financial reports for the company, including Q4 adjusted earnings and sales, and a revised earning outlook for 2026.

feed.title: Earnings Flash (ADI) Analog Devices, Inc. Reports Q1 Revenue $3.16B, vs. FactSet Est of $3.12B
feed.summary: Analog Devices, Inc. (ADI) reported first-quarter revenue of $3.16 billion, surpassing the FactSet estimate of $3.12 billion. The company also announced Q1 adjusted EPS of $2.46 per share, which exceeded the FactSet estimate of $2.41. Additionally, Analog Devices raised its quarterly dividend by 11%.

feed.title: JPMorgan Chase & Co. Grows Stock Position in Altria Group, Inc. $MO
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. increased its stake in Altria Group, Inc. by 7.2% during the third quarter, bringing its total holdings to over 12 million shares worth approximately $797.59 million. Other institutional investors also adjusted their positions in Altria, which recently reported an earnings per share of $1.30, slightly missing analyst estimates, and announced a new $2.00 billion stock repurchase program. The company also declared a quarterly dividend of $1.06 per share, and analysts currently maintain a "Hold" consensus rating with a target price of $63.67.

feed.title: JPMorgan Chase & Co. Has $645.83 Million Stake in Leidos Holdings, Inc. $LDOS
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. reduced its stake in Leidos Holdings, Inc. by 3.1% in Q3, now holding 3,417,810 shares valued at $645.83 million. Other institutional investors also adjusted their positions, with 76.12% of the stock owned by institutions. Leidos recently reported strong Q4 results, topping EPS estimates, and announced a quarterly dividend, despite a revenue miss attributed to a government shutdown.

feed.title: Zacks Research Has Negative Outlook for ARE Q1 Earnings
feed.summary: Zacks Research has lowered its Q1 2026 EPS estimate for Alexandria Real Estate Equities (ARE) to $1.74, maintaining a "Hold" rating, and projecting a full-year 2026 EPS of $6.39. This follows ARE's last reported quarterly EPS of $2.16, which beat estimates, despite a 4.4% year-over-year revenue decline. The company has also authorized a $500 million share buyback and declared a quarterly dividend of $0.72.

feed.title: Vulcan Materials Stock Outlook: Is Wall Street Bullish or Bearish?
feed.summary: Vulcan Materials Company (VMC) has underperformed the S&P 500 over the past year, gaining 11.7% compared to the S&P 500's 11.9%. The company's Q4 2025 results and 2026 guidance were weaker than expected, causing a 5.5% stock drop. Despite this, analysts have a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating for VMC, with an average price target that suggests a 9.5% premium to its current levels.

feed.title: Earnings Flash (PODD) Insulet Corporation Reports Q4 Revenue $783.8M, vs. FactSet Est of $769.0M
feed.summary: Insulet Corporation (PODD) has reported its Q4 revenue, reaching $783.8 million, surpassing FactSet's estimated $769.0 million. Additionally, the company announced Q4 adjusted EPS of $1.55 per share, exceeding FactSet's estimate of $1.46 per share. This financial performance indicates stronger-than-expected results for the medical device company.

feed.title: PNC Financial Services Group Inc. Sells 9,637 Shares of Aercap Holdings N.V. $AER
feed.summary: PNC Financial Services Group Inc. reduced its stake in Aercap Holdings N.V. by 6.3% in the third quarter, selling 9,637 shares and now owning 142,533 shares valued at $17.25 million. Other institutional investors also adjusted their positions in the stock. Aercap recently announced a quarterly dividend increase to $0.40 per share and reported strong earnings, beating analyst expectations with $3.95 EPS on $2.24 billion revenue.

feed.title: Fifth Third Bancorp Sells 4,675 Shares of Hershey Company (The) $HSY
feed.summary: Fifth Third Bancorp recently reduced its stake in Hershey Company by selling 4,675 shares, lowering its holdings to 40,548 shares valued at $7.585 million. Other institutional investors like SJS Investment Consulting and Vermillion & White Wealth Management Group also adjusted their positions, while corporate insiders CEO Kirk Tanner and CFO Steven E. Voskuil engaged in buying and selling activity, respectively. Analysts have a "Hold" consensus rating on Hershey, with recent price target adjustments and a declared quarterly dividend increase.

feed.title: Shell Asset Management Co. Sells 14,542 Shares of Corteva, Inc. $CTVA
feed.summary: Shell Asset Management Co. reduced its stake in Corteva, Inc. by 64.3% in the third quarter, selling 14,542 shares and retaining 8,058 shares worth $545,000. Despite this, other institutional investors like Vanguard Group Inc. and Geode Capital Management LLC increased their holdings. Corteva recently reported earnings of $0.22 per share, matching consensus, but revenue of $3.91 billion missed analyst estimates, and the company announced a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share.

feed.title: Cummins Inc. $CMI Shares Sold by Shell Asset Management Co.
feed.summary: Shell Asset Management Co. significantly reduced its stake in Cummins Inc. by 88.2% in the third quarter, selling 4,860 shares valued at $274,000. Despite this, other major institutional investors like Vanguard Group Inc., State Street Corp, and Fisher Asset Management LLC increased their holdings in Cummins during the second quarter. The article also notes recent insider sales by Brett Michael Merritt and William I. Miller, and several analysts have recently raised their price targets for CMI, with a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating.

feed.title: Analog Devices Stock Rises After Earnings. Chip Maker’s Strong Year Continues.
feed.summary: Analog Devices saw its stock rise after reporting stronger-than-expected fiscal first-quarter earnings. The chip maker also provided robust guidance for the current second quarter. This performance continues a strong start to 2026 for Analog Devices and other analog semiconductor companies.

feed.title: Global Payments appoints former Albertsons CEO to board of directors
feed.summary: Global Payments Inc. announced the appointment of Vivek Sankaran, former CEO of Albertsons Companies, to its Board of Directors, effective February 19, 2026. Sankaran brings significant experience in technology and digital transformations from his roles at Albertsons and PepsiCo. This appointment follows Global Payments' recent acquisition of Worldpay and divestiture of its Issuer Solutions business, signaling a strategic focus on commerce solutions.

feed.title: ANALOG DEVICES INC SEC 10-Q Report
feed.summary: Analog Devices Inc. (ADI) has released its Form 10-Q report for Q1 fiscal 2026, showcasing significant financial growth with a 30% increase in revenue to $3,160.3 million and a 112% rise in net income to $830.826 million. The company reported strong performance across its Industrial, Automotive, Communications, and Consumer segments, leading to improved gross margins and operational efficiency. ADI anticipates continued growth and innovation, supported by ongoing R&D investments and projected capital expenditures.

feed.title: Dominion Energy Rose 5% This Week. Here’s Why the Stock Could Climb Further in 2026
feed.summary: Dominion Energy's stock rose 5% this week, trading near $66 per share, driven by updates on its Coastal Virginia Offshore Wind project and institutional buying. Despite a slight increase in project costs and a delay in completion, investor focus remains on the company's regulated earnings and long-term infrastructure expansion. Analyst models project a target price of $77 by the end of 2027, indicating a potential 16.6% upside, suggesting the stock is currently undervalued.

feed.title: Aberdeen Group plc Acquires 644,930 Shares of Kenvue Inc. $KVUE
feed.summary: Aberdeen Group plc significantly increased its stake in Kenvue Inc. (NYSE:KVUE) by 84.1%, acquiring an additional 644,930 shares, bringing its total to 1,411,380 shares valued at approximately $22.9 million. This comes as Kenvue beat Q4 earnings and revenue estimates, reporting $0.27 EPS and $3.78 billion in revenue, and announced a quarterly dividend. However, the company's high payout ratio and recent workforce reductions are noted as potential concerns, while institutional investors like CWM LLC and Allianz Asset Management GmbH also modified their holdings.

feed.title: Ball Corporation $BALL Stake Boosted by Assetmark Inc.
feed.summary: Assetmark Inc. significantly increased its stake in Ball Corporation (NYSE:BALL) by 34.4% in the third quarter, adding 27,994 shares to its holdings, which are now valued at $5.512 million. Other institutional investors also adjusted their positions in Ball, with several firms acquiring new stakes or substantially increasing their existing ones. The company has a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts, with various firms setting price targets ranging from $60.00 to $75.00.

feed.title: Advisors Asset Management Inc. Has $913,000 Stock Holdings in KKR & Co. Inc. $KKR
feed.summary: Advisors Asset Management Inc. reduced its stake in KKR & Co. Inc. by 59.2% in the third quarter, holding 7,024 shares valued at $913,000. Despite this, other institutions like Waterway Wealth Management LLC and First National Trust Co. increased their holdings in KKR. Analysts have a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating for KKR with a target price of $156.57, and a director recently purchased 50,000 shares.

feed.title: Moody`s Corp. Reports Advance In Q4 Profit
feed.summary: Moody's Corp. (MCO) announced a significant increase in its Q4 profit, with earnings rising to $610 million, or $3.41 per share, compared to $395 million, or $2.17 per share, last year. Excluding certain items, adjusted earnings were $651 million, or $3.64 per share. The company's revenue also saw a 13.0% increase, reaching $1.889 billion for the quarter.

feed.title: Aberdeen Group plc Buys 34,122 Shares of Jacobs Solutions Inc. $J
feed.summary: Aberdeen Group plc increased its stake in Jacobs Solutions Inc. by 23.7%, acquiring an additional 34,122 shares, bringing their total to 178,002 shares valued at $26.52 million. Other institutional investors also adjusted their holdings in Jacobs Solutions. The company recently reported Q3 EPS of $1.53, beating estimates, and revenue of $2.30 billion, missing consensus, while raising its quarterly dividend and receiving a "Moderate Buy" average analyst rating.

feed.title: AcuityMD Announces AcuityMD for Salesforce on Salesforce AppExchange
feed.summary: AcuityMD has launched "AcuityMD for Salesforce" on the Salesforce AppExchange, enabling MedTech commercial teams to integrate AcuityMD's platform directly with Salesforce. This integration allows sales representatives to streamline their workflows, identify opportunities more efficiently, and keep data consistent across systems, ultimately helping them achieve sales goals faster. The partnership aims to enhance efficiency and visibility for MedTech companies by aligning targeting, account, and opportunity data within a single commercial workflow.

feed.title: US FDA to initiate review of Moderna's influenza vaccine
feed.summary: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration has agreed to review Moderna's influenza vaccine after initially refusing due to trial design flaws. Moderna will seek full approval for adults aged 50-64 and accelerated approval for those 65 and above, with a decision expected by August 5. The company will also conduct an additional post-marketing study for older adults.

feed.title: GARMIN LTD SEC 10-K Report
feed.summary: Garmin Ltd. has released its annual 10-K report, revealing strong financial performance with a 15% increase in net sales to $7,245.5 million and an 18% rise in net income to $1,663.9 million for the fiscal year. The company demonstrated robust growth across its fitness and outdoor segments, supported by innovative product launches and strategic acquisitions. Despite successes, Garmin faces challenges including geopolitical risks, supply chain dependencies, and cybersecurity threats, but plans to continue its focus on innovation and market expansion.

feed.title: Charles River names Glenn Coleman as CFO, Kerry Dailey as CLO
feed.summary: Charles River Laboratories International Inc. (NYSE: CRL) has announced two significant executive appointments. Glenn G. Coleman is set to join as the new Chief Financial Officer, bringing over 30 years of financial management experience, while Kerry Dailey will take on the newly created role of Chief Legal Officer, leading the global legal and compliance functions. These appointments follow the interim CFO Michael G. Knell, and current General Counsel Matthew L. Daniel's planned departure.

feed.title: Amazon.com Announces Fourth Quarter Results; Andy Jassy Comments
feed.summary: Amazon.com, Inc. announced its fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 financial results, highlighting strong growth in its grocery and food-related operations, including expanded same-day grocery delivery. CEO Andy Jassy emphasized rapid innovation across various segments, contributing to significant year-over-year sales increases. The company also detailed operational achievements in delivery speed and expansion of its grocery services to more than 2,300 cities and towns.

feed.title: Kroger to Invest $23 Million in Parkaire Landing Expansion
feed.summary: Kroger is investing $23 million to expand its store in the Parkaire Landing shopping center in East Cobb. The expansion will add over 26,000 square feet, enhancing the deli, produce, and adding a sushi kiosk and Murray's cheese counter. Construction is underway, with completion expected in Spring 2027, and the store will remain open throughout the process.

feed.title: Global Payments Q4 profit beats; announces $2.5-billion share repurchase authorization
feed.summary: Global Payments (GPN) reported better-than-expected fourth-quarter profits. The company also announced a new $2.5 billion share repurchase authorization. This news is expected to be positive for the stock.

feed.title: US-based semiconductor company Qualcomm to invest $150 million in India's AI startup ecosystem
feed.summary: Qualcomm has announced a $150 million investment to bolster India's AI startup ecosystem through its venture capital arm, Qualcomm Ventures. The focus areas for investment include AI in automotive, IoT, robotics, and mobile, coinciding with CEO Cristiano Amon's participation in the India AI Impact Summit. This initiative deepens Qualcomm's almost two-decade presence in India, supporting the country's growing role in global AI innovation.

feed.title: Garmin Ltd. Reports Record Fourth Quarter and Fiscal Year 2025 Results
feed.summary: Garmin Ltd. announced record financial results for the fourth quarter and fiscal year 2025, with consolidated revenue reaching $7.25 billion for the year and a 15% increase from the prior year. The company reported strong performance across its fitness, aviation, and marine segments, leading to a proposed 17% dividend increase and a new $500 million share repurchase program. For fiscal year 2026, Garmin anticipates continued growth, projecting $7.9 billion in revenue and a pro forma EPS of $9.35.

feed.title: Uber to invest over $100 million in autonomous vehicle charging amid robotaxi push
feed.summary: Uber is committing over $100 million to build autonomous vehicle charging hubs to support its expanding robotaxi operations. This investment includes DC fast charging stations at its depots and strategic pit stops in priority cities, starting with the Bay Area, Los Angeles, and Dallas in the U.S. The company is also forming "utilization guarantee agreements" with chargepoint operators globally to accelerate the rollout of new chargers in key metropolitan areas.

feed.title: Two new chiefs at Charles River Labs to steer finance and legal
feed.summary: Charles River Laboratories International, Inc. (NYSE: CRL) has announced two key executive appointments to strengthen its leadership team. Glenn G. Coleman will join as the new Chief Financial Officer, bringing over 30 years of financial and operational management experience in the healthcare sector. Kerry Dailey has been named to the newly-created position of Chief Legal Officer, with extensive expertise in advising multinational life science companies on legal and regulatory matters.

feed.title: Analog Devices Reports Fiscal First Quarter 2026 Financial Results
feed.summary: Analog Devices (ADI) announced robust financial results for its fiscal first quarter ended January 31, 2026, with revenue reaching $3.16 billion, demonstrating year-over-year growth across all end markets, led by Industrial and Communications. The company generated $5.1 billion in operating cash flow and $4.6 billion in free cash flow on a trailing twelve-month basis and returned $1.0 billion to shareholders. ADI also raised its quarterly dividend by 11% to $1.10, marking twenty-two consecutive years of increases, and provided an optimistic revenue outlook of $3.5 billion for the second quarter of fiscal 2026.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley’s 2025 Top 50 Watch Brands Report: Rolex Leads Amid Rising Market Polarisation
feed.summary: The Morgan Stanley and LuxeConsult 2025 report on the Swiss watch industry reveals continued market contraction and strong polarization, with the top 4 brands accounting for over 50% of the market. Rolex maintains its leading position, despite a slight volume decline, while many other brands, especially those in the mid-tier, are struggling. The report also highlights the increasing premiumization of the market, with ultra-luxury watches driving growth despite low volumes.

feed.title: Charles River Laboratories Announces Fourth-Quarter and Full-Year 2025 Results and Provides 2026 Guidance
feed.summary: Charles River Laboratories (NYSE: CRL) reported its Q4 and full-year 2025 financial results, showing a slight decrease in revenue but significant non-cash impairment charges impacting GAAP earnings. Despite the revenue dip, the company is cautiously optimistic for 2026, anticipating a return to organic revenue growth in the second half of the year due to stabilized biopharmaceutical demand and strategic initiatives, including a focus on efficiency and targeted acquisitions. The 2026 guidance projects non-GAAP earnings per share to increase by approximately 4% to 9%.

feed.title: Animal health shake-up: Covetrus and MWI plan $3.5B merger
feed.summary: Cencora's MWI Animal Health and Covetrus are set to merge in a $3.5 billion deal, creating a comprehensive animal health platform. This combination aims to enhance access to animal healthcare, drive operational efficiencies for veterinary practices, and support innovation in the industry. Cencora will receive $1.25 billion in cash, $800 million in preferred equity, and a 34.3% common equity stake in the new combined entity.

feed.title: As Walmart and Target head in different directions, all eyes are on their new CEOs
feed.summary: Walmart and Target are both under new CEO leadership, with John Furner at Walmart and Michael Fiddelke at Target. While Furner aims to maintain Walmart's recent strong performance and integrate AI, Fiddelke faces the challenge of revitalizing Target amidst declining sales and store traffic. Investors will closely watch their respective earnings reports for insights into their strategies for the future.

feed.title: Uber Will Offer Incentives for E.V. Charger Construction
feed.summary: Uber announced incentives for companies to install EV chargers in the U.S. and Europe, particularly in areas frequented by its drivers. This initiative aims to address the challenges of predicting charger usage and investment return, especially after recent U.S. policy changes that have impacted EV sales. Ride-share drivers, who are heavy users of public charging stations, are seen as crucial to the growth and viability of the charging infrastructure.

feed.title: Philip Morris International Presents at 2026 CAGNY Conference; Reaffirms 2026 Full-Year Forecast
feed.summary: Philip Morris International (PMI) is presenting at the 2026 Consumer Analyst Group of New York (CAGNY) Conference, with its Group CEO and CFO addressing investors. The company is reaffirming its 2026 full-year diluted EPS forecast, projecting adjusted diluted EPS growth of 11.1% to 13.1% (or 7.5% to 9.5% excluding currency impact). PMI also highlighted its transformation, leadership in smoke-free brands, and commitment to delivering superior shareholder returns and long-term growth targets.

feed.title: Moderna’s mRNA flu shot heads to FDA review, aiming at adults over 50
feed.summary: Moderna announced that the FDA has accepted its biologics license application for the seasonal influenza vaccine candidate mRNA-1010, setting a PDUFA goal date of August 5, 2026. The company proposes a regulatory pathway seeking full approval for adults aged 50-64 and accelerated approval for those 65+, with a post-marketing study in older adults. mRNA-1010 is also under review in Europe, Canada, and Australia, with potential first approvals expected in 2026.

feed.title: Altria Presents as Part of the Consumer Analyst Group of New York Conference; Reaffirms 2026 Full-Year Earnings Guidance
feed.summary: Altria Group, Inc. is participating in the Consumer Analyst Group of New York Conference, where its CEO and CFO will highlight the company's smoke-free portfolio and long-term growth aspirations. The company reaffirmed its 2026 full-year adjusted diluted EPS guidance in a range of $5.56 to $5.72, representing a growth rate of 2.5% to 5.5% from a 2025 base of $5.42. Altria's guidance accounts for investments in contract manufacturing, limited impact on volumes from illicit enforcement, and NJOY ACE not returning to the market in 2026.

feed.title: Global Payments Announces Board Appointment
feed.summary: Global Payments Inc. announced the appointment of Vivek Sankaran as a new independent director to its Board of Directors, effective February 19, 2026. This appointment follows constructive engagement with Elliott Investment Management L.P. Sankaran, known for his leadership in technology and digital transformations at companies like Albertsons and PepsiCo, will bring valuable expertise as Global Payments integrates Worldpay and continues its strategic vision.

feed.title: Garmin announces fourth quarter and fiscal year 2025 results
feed.summary: Garmin Ltd. announced record financial results for both the fourth quarter and full fiscal year 2025, with consolidated revenue reaching $2.12 billion for the quarter and $7.25 billion for the year. This strong performance was driven by growth across all segments, particularly Fitness, and the company proposed a 17% dividend increase along with a new $500 million share repurchase program. Garmin also provided positive guidance for fiscal year 2026, projecting $7.9 billion in revenue and $9.35 in pro forma EPS.

feed.title: CoreWeave, Inc. (CRWV) Investors: March 13, 2026 Filing Deadline in Securities Class Action - Contact Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP
feed.summary: Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP has filed a securities fraud class action lawsuit against CoreWeave, Inc. (NASDAQ: CRWV) on behalf of investors who purchased securities between March 28, 2025, and December 15, 2025. The lawsuit alleges that CoreWeave made materially false and misleading statements regarding its ability to meet customer demand and its reliance on a single third-party data center supplier. Investors affected by these alleged misstatements have until March 13, 2026, to seek appointment as a lead plaintiff in the case.

feed.title: Global Payments Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Results
feed.summary: Global Payments Inc. announced its fourth quarter and full year 2025 financial results, highlighting strong performance driven by the acquisition of Worldpay and the divestiture of Issuer Solutions, repositioning the company as a pure-play merchant solutions provider. The company reported adjusted EPS of $3.18 for Q4 2025 and $12.22 for the full year, along with increases in adjusted net revenue. Global Payments also announced a $2.5 billion share repurchase authorization, including a $550 million accelerated share repurchase plan, and provided a positive outlook for 2026 with anticipated adjusted EPS growth of 13% to 15%.

feed.title: Raymond James Initiates Coverage on VTOL with Outperform Rating
feed.summary: Raymond James has initiated coverage on Bristow Group (VTOL) with an "Outperform" rating and a price target of $60.00. This positive endorsement follows a previous "Buy" rating from Jones Trading in January 2026. While analysts forecast an average target price of $52.33 with an 18.40% upside, GuruFocus estimates the fair value at $36.91, suggesting a potential downside.

feed.title: Waste Management Inc (NYSE:WM) Emerges as a Technical Breakout Candidate
feed.summary: Waste Management Inc (WM) has been identified as a technical breakout candidate through a scan filtering for stocks with strong technical ratings and consolidating price patterns. The company exhibits a solid uptrend and a high-quality setup for a potential breakout, characterized by clear resistance and support levels. Investors using a breakout strategy are advised to watch WM for a move above the $235.51 resistance level.
